title: fraxinus pennsylvanica var. viridis
intro: Fraxinus pennsylvanica var. viridis, also known as the Green Ash or Green American Ash, is a deciduous tree that belongs to the Oleaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to the presence of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Fraxinus pennsylvanica var. viridis include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce pain and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of the tree's bark and leaves can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for internal use, while the bark can also be used to make a topical ointment for skin conditions.
